name: Overview / Slide-Sorter Intent
summary: A spatial zoom-out to a grid of all slides — the slide-sorter — where the user scans the whole deck at once, selects a slide to jump to it, and optionally drags to reorder. Distinct from the Navigation intent: navigation is hierarchical/lateral/linear traversal between views, whereas this is a spatial overview of equivalent ordered items (it composes collection-operations for the grid, selection, and reorder). The always-on core is select-to-jump; reorder is the authoring layer. Homed in webintents per the ratified distributed placement (#1175).

grouping.description: How the grid arranges slides. flat = one grid of every slide in order; sectioned = grids grouped by the deck's sections (mirrors the document model's deck > sections > slides).

reorder.description: Whether the overview supports drag-reorder. none = read-only overview (navigate only — the presenter/viewer case); drag = drag-to-reorder slides (the authoring case), composing collection-operations move semantics.

description:
Zoom out to see the whole deck

The slide-sorter: a spatial overview where every slide is shrunk into a grid so the user can scan the entire deck at once, click a slide to jump straight to it, and — when authoring — drag to reorder. Every desktop deck tool ships this view; on the web it is a distinct interaction mode, not just a smaller render.

Spatial, not hierarchical

This is deliberately not the Navigation intent. Navigation models hierarchical/lateral/linear view changes with history and URL semantics; the overview is a spatial rearrangement of equivalent ordered items. It composes Collection Operations for the grid layout, selection, and drag-reorder.

Core vs layer

  • Always on: select-to-jump — tapping a thumbnail navigates to that slide.
  • Authoring layer: reorder: drag mutates slide order in the document model.
